On this page

  • Overview

Introduction

neo-llvm is a production-ready Rust toolchain for compiling Neo N3 smart contracts. It takes standard Rust code, compiles it to WebAssembly, then translates the Wasm bytecode into NeoVM instructions — producing the NEF binary and manifest JSON that Neo N3 nodes expect.

Compilation Pipeline

  1. You write a smart contract in Rust using the rust-devpack SDK.
  2. cargo build compiles it to a .wasm binary targeting wasm32-unknown-unknown.
  3. The wasm-neovm translator reads the Wasm module and emits NeoVM opcodes.
  4. The output is a .nef file (the on-chain bytecode) and a .manifest.json file (the contract metadata).

Why Rust?

Neo N3 contracts are traditionally written in C#. Rust offers a different set of trade-offs:

Rust (neo-llvm)C# (neo-devpack-dotnet)
Memory safetyCompile-time guarantees via ownershipGarbage-collected runtime
EcosystemCargo, crates.io, mature toolingNuGet, .NET SDK
PerformanceZero-cost abstractionsJIT-compiled
Learning curveSteeper, but enforces correctnessGentler for .NET developers
Contract sizeTypically smaller Wasm outputManaged IL output

Both toolchains produce valid NEF bytecode. Choose the one that fits your team and project.

What’s Included

wasm-neovm

The core translator. Reads Wasm modules and emits NeoVM bytecode as NEF binaries with accompanying manifest files.

rust-devpack

The Rust SDK — types (NeoInteger, NeoByteString, NeoStorage), macros (#[neo_contract], #[neo_method], #[neo_event]), runtime bindings, and syscall wrappers.

17 Example Contracts

From hello-world to Uniswap V2 AMMs, flashloan pools, NFT marketplaces, governance DAOs, and more. Every example compiles, tests, and produces valid NEF output.

Cross-Chain Layers

Experimental compatibility layers for Solana (solana-compat) and Move (move-neovm), enabling developers from other ecosystems to target NeoVM.

Project Status

  • Version: 0.4.7
  • License: MIT
  • Neo N3 compatibility: 3.9.1
  • Test coverage: 500+ tests across unit, integration, and contract-level suites
  • CI: GitHub Actions with Neo Express integration testing

Next Steps

  • Install the toolchain to set up your development environment.
  • Follow the Quick Start to compile your first contract end-to-end.
  • Explore the project structure to understand how the repository is organized.
